finding_results.notes: Tomás now raises two specific objections rooted in his experience of the fall: (1) the suddenness vs Monday's gradual onset (line 93: 'Monday was different, though. Monday it came on slow. This one just hit.') and (2) his legs feeling fast right before the drop (line 97: 'But my legs felt fast. Right before. They felt really good.'). Margot must evade twice with substitute truths — a fabricated altitude-threshold explanation (line 95) and a system-masking-oxygen-deficit cover (line 99). The trainer exchange now pushes harder: he quotes the specific peak (161), asks for the target ceiling, does the math on the overshoot (line 127-131), and asks what happened at seven. Margot admits to the four-beat mid-session bump but frames it as diagnostic (line 133). The trainer's flat confirmation prompt 'Did it.' (line 135) holds the exchange open and forces Margot to confirm the lie explicitly (line 137: 'At seven. Yeah.'). The medical-form advice was revised from conspiratorial framing to practical self-interest (line 107). The cafeteria scene's reference to Tomás dropping 'the heaviness arriving all at once' from his retelling (line 247) is now better grounded by his having explicitly raised that detail with Margot in the revised exchange.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 4 was rebuilt around a stronger concealment chain: Margot's routine telemetry abuse is now visibly patterned, Tomas's fall produces a forced discrepancy and publicized lie instead of only a false report, repeated cross-chapter room setup was removed, and the chapter now clears the 3500-word minimum.

Revised the post-fall dialogue exchange to add meaningful verbal resistance before Margot's cover story is accepted. Tomás now pushes back twice with specific observations from the run (the suddenness of the drop vs Monday, and his legs feeling fast right before). The trainer now challenges with telemetry specifics — quoting the peak, asking for the target ceiling, noting the eleven-beat overshoot, and prompting Margot to confirm her diagnostic rationale. Margot's evasion style (substitute truth) is preserved: she offers plausible coaching science that diverts from the real cause without constructing elaborate lies. All voices maintained per style bible: Tomás's speech is simple, physical, and slightly careful under stress; Margot is direct and blunt; the trainer is numbers-first and professional. No changes outside the targeted dialogue passages.

Revised chapter_04 to resolve the access-control exposition finding by embedding the concealment mechanics inside Margot's close-third narration, while preserving the chapter's engine, Tomas's trust, and the telemetry trail that can later expose her pattern. Also normalized Tomas's name spelling in this chapter to match the continuity sheet.
